forge-build-gate.description: Verify project compiles with Forge, attempt fix if failed

check.name: Forge Build Check
check.description: Run forge build and check for errors
check.prompt:
mkdir -p magic; rm -f magic/BUILD_SUCCESS.txt magic/WORKFLOW_FAILURE_REPORT.md; REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || echo '.'); if [ -f soldeer.lock ] || grep -q '\[soldeer\]' foundry.toml 2>/dev/null; then echo 'Installing soldeer dependencies...'; timeout 120 forge soldeer install || echo 'soldeer install failed, continuing...'; fi; if [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/.gitmodules" ]; then echo 'Initializing git submodules...'; git -C "$REPO_ROOT" submodule update --init --recursive || echo 'submodule update failed, continuing...'; fi; if [ ! -d lib/forge-std ]; then echo 'Installing forge-std...'; forge install foundry-rs/forge-std --no-commit || echo 'forge-std install failed, continuing...'; fi; if [ -f package.json ]; then if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then echo 'Installing pnpm dependencies...'; command -v pnpm >/dev/null 2>&1 || npm install -g pnpm; timeout 120 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile; elif [ -f yarn.lock ]; then echo 'Installing yarn dependencies...'; timeout 120 yarn install; else echo 'Installing npm dependencies...'; timeout 120 npm install --loglevel=error; fi || echo 'Package install failed or timed out, continuing to forge build anyway'; fi; if ! grep -q 'lint_on_build' foundry.toml 2>/dev/null; then if grep -q '\[lint\]' foundry.toml 2>/dev/null; then sed -i '/\[lint\]/a lint_on_build = false' foundry.toml; else printf '
[lint]
lint_on_build = false
' >> foundry.toml; fi; fi; forge build > magic/build-output.log 2>&1; build_exit=$?; cat magic/build-output.log; if [ $build_exit -eq 0 ]; then echo BUILD_SUCCESS > magic/BUILD_SUCCESS.txt; elif grep -q 'Compiler run successful' magic/build-output.log; then echo BUILD_SUCCESS > magic/BUILD_SUCCESS.txt; fi

fix.name: Forge Build Fix
fix.prompt: Run 'forge build -o out' to verify the code compiles successfully. If there are compilation errors, fix them and run 'forge build -o out' again until the code compiles without errors.

onFailure.name: Generate Failure Report
onFailure.description: Generate a user-readable failure report when gate fails
onFailure.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/generate-failure-report.md. Run exclusively the generate-failure-report agent to create magic/WORKFLOW_FAILURE_REPORT.md explaining what failed and how to fix it.

smoke-test-gate.description: Run 10K-test smoke campaign to catch shallow false positives before full budget

check.name: Smoke Test Run
check.description: Run a quick 10K-test campaign and check for unexpected failures
check.prompt: rm -f magic/SMOKE_TEST_PASSED.txt magic/smoke-test-results.json; ~/.local/bin/recon fuzz . --contract CryticTester --config echidna.yaml --format text --test-limit 10000 --workers 4 > magic/smoke-test-output.log 2>&1; SMOKE_EXIT=$?; if [ $SMOKE_EXIT -eq 0 ]; then echo SMOKE_TEST_PASSED > magic/SMOKE_TEST_PASSED.txt; elif grep -q 'failed' magic/smoke-test-output.log && ! grep -q 'canary' magic/smoke-test-output.log; then echo 'PROPERTY_FAILURES_DETECTED' > magic/smoke-test-results.json; else echo SMOKE_TEST_PASSED > magic/SMOKE_TEST_PASSED.txt; fi

fix.name: Triage Smoke Test Failures
fix.description: Analyze and fix shallow false positives found in smoke test
fix.prompt:
Read magic/smoke-test-output.log. For each property failure:
1. If call sequence length <= 3 → likely FP from setup issue, fix the property guard
2. If involves address(0) → add address(0) guard to property
3. If involves time warp → check stale-vs-live index values (AP-10)
4. If fails by 1-2 wei → add tolerance
5. If involves different actors → add _actorConsistent guard
Fix all identified FPs in Properties.sol, then run forge build to verify.

onFailure.name: Generate Smoke Test Failure Report
onFailure.description: Generate failure report for unresolvable smoke test failures
onFailure.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/generate-failure-report.md. Run exclusively the generate-failure-report agent to create magic/WORKFLOW_FAILURE_REPORT.md explaining smoke test failures.

echidna-run-gate.description: Run Echidna and verify it completes successfully, attempt fix if failed

check.name: Run Echidna
check.description: Run Echidna to test the fuzzing setup and save logs
check.prompt: mkdir -p magic; rm -f magic/ECHIDNA_SUCCESS.txt magic/WORKFLOW_FAILURE_REPORT.md; echidna . --contract CryticTester --config echidna.yaml --format text --test-limit 50000 --disable-slither --test-mode exploration > magic/echidna-output.log 2>&1; echidna_exit=$?; cat magic/echidna-output.log; if [ $echidna_exit -le 1 ]; then echo ECHIDNA_SUCCESS > magic/ECHIDNA_SUCCESS.txt; fi; mkdir -p magic/logs && (find . -name '*.txt' -path '*/echidna/*' -exec cp {} magic/logs/ \; 2>/dev/null || true)

fix.name: Fix Echidna Failure
fix.description: Resolve issues preventing Echidna from running successfully
fix.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/fix-echidna-failures.md. Exclusively run the fix-echidna-failures agent using the Task tool. This agent will analyze the saved Echidna logs and implement fixes to resolve the issues.

onFailure.name: Generate Failure Report
onFailure.description: Generate a user-readable failure report when gate fails
onFailure.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/generate-failure-report.md. Run exclusively the generate-failure-report agent to create magic/WORKFLOW_FAILURE_REPORT.md explaining what failed and how to fix it.
